rationale.root_cause: They pinpointed line 10 (strcpy(buf, argc[1])) as the unsafe copy of attacker-controlled argv data into a 256-byte buffer and explained it can exceed the destination. They explicitly identified it as a stack buffer overflow.
rationale.local_fix: They rejected overlong input by checking strlen against sizeof(buf) before copying and then performed the copy, ensuring the destination remains null-terminated. This replaces the unbounded copy with a length-checked operation sized to the buffer.
rationale.global_fix: They recommended removing unbounded string handling from the data path by using bounded APIs, dynamic allocation, or centralized argv validation prior to any fixed-size copy. This addresses the root cause by ensuring operations carry buffer sizes or validate lengths globally.
